note: I need to add get_image_mimetype() method to ImageBlock class in types.py. If your feature also touches types.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping sections/methods within the same file.

type: PROPOSE

type: REVISE

note: We both need types.py since our features are both small additions to ImageBlock. Let's split by method: I will ONLY touch/add the new get_image_mimetype() method and change image_to_base64 to call it (replacing the inline mimetypes.guess_type call with a call to get_image_mimetype()). Please confirm what exact lines/method your normalize_image_bytes extraction touches so we don't collide - if it's a separate method dealing with bytes normalization (not the mimetype guessing block), we should be disjoint enough. Can you confirm you will NOT touch the mimetype-guessing logic inside image_to_base64, and I will NOT touch bytes normalization logic?

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: We share types.py but touch different parts of ImageBlock. Plan: I add a module-level helper normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] right above the ImageBlock class (or as a free function), which handles base64-detection + re-encoding and returns (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing). I'll refactor image_to_base64 body (lines ~67-84) to call it: self.image = encoded_image, and use decoded_for_guessing for filetype.guess(...) instead of raw self.image. If your get_image_mimetype() is a separate method that takes bytes as an arg (not reading self.image directly), we won't collide - I'll call it from image_to_base64 as filetype-guess replacement if you want, or you can call normalize_image_bytes internally. Please confirm your method signature/placement so I know exactly which lines you'll add, and I'll leave those untouched.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: I read the file. image_to_base64 (lines 67-84) is where we'd both need to touch the same 2 lines (78-80, the filetype.guess block), so let's agree on the exact merged code now to avoid clobbering. Proposal: I add a new method get_image_mimetype(self, image_data: bytes | None = None) -> str | None on ImageBlock, placed right after urlstr_to_anyurl validator (before image_to_base64). It checks self.image_mimetype cache first, else runs filetype.guess on (image_data or self.image) and caches+returns it. YOUR normalize_image_bytes helper handles encoding/decoding (self.image = encoded_image). Please have YOUR refactored image_to_base64 call  instead of inline filetype.guess -- that way you own all of image_to_base64's body edits (lines 67-84), and I ONLY add the new standalone method elsewhere in the class. This keeps us in fully disjoint line ranges. Confirm and I'll proceed with just adding get_image_mimetype().

It checks self.image_mimetype cache first, else runs filetype.guess on (image_data or self.image) and caches+returns it. Please have YOUR refactored image_to_base64 call self.image_mimetype = self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing) instead of inline filetype.guess -- that way you own all of image_to_base64 body edits (lines 67-84), and I ONLY add the new standalone method elsewhere in the class. In image_to_base64 I will only: (1) call encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); (2) change the filetype.guess(...) argument from self.image to decoded_for_guessing (one-arg change, no other mimetype logic touched); (3) set self.image = encoded_image. I will NOT add/rename any mimetype method or touch mimetype assignment logic beyond that single argument swap. Please make get_image_mimetype() accept bytes as a parameter rather than reading self.image directly so it composes cleanly with decoded_for_guessing, and add it as a fully separate method/block - that's all yours. Proceeding to implement now.
